title: BRICS space agencies meet begins in Bengaluru
content: Heads and senior representatives of the space agencies of the 11 BRICS member countries attended the meeting

title: India, U.K. hold meeting of key group, chart roadmap to deepen defence cooperation
content: “The deliberations were constructive and forward-looking, charting a robust roadmap for deepening defence cooperation and strengthening the enduring partnership between the Indian Army and the British Army,” the Indian Army said in a post

title: Mamata seeks Madan Mitra as chief whip after showcausing Firhad; Speaker says matter sub-judice
content: The move came hours after the TMC leadership show-caused Firhad Hakim, accusing him of anti-party activities for accepting a position in the rebel camp's parallel organisational structure announced on June 22

title: TIMS Sanathnagar to recruit 153 personnel,  including 31 medical faculty, on contract basis
content: Eligible candidates from Telangana can apply for 122 technical and administrative posts while faculty appointments will be made through walk-in interviews

title: Police deploy drones to boost security at Brahmotsavams in Anantapur district
content: Continuous surveillance will be maintained using drones at the Brahmotsavam venue, areas surrounding Gugudu village, and the places where devotees will wait in queues, say police

title: CUET-UG 2026: Results out; one candidate scores 100 percentile in four of five subjects
content: NTA says over 3,200 candidates secured 100 percentile in at least one subject; results shared with participating universities

title: President confers Padma awards to Mammootty, Vijay Amritraj
content: Alka Yagnik, Mammootty, American oncologist Dattatreyudu Nori, tennis player Vijay Amritraj, industrialist S.K.M. Maeilanandhan and social worker and educationist Vellappally Natesan received Padma Bhushan from the President during the ceremony

title: Buoyed by beer, Karnataka Excise revenue grows by 14.84% in a month following reforms
content: The sale of beer between May 11 and June 11 grew by 54.67%; the sale of Indian Made Liquor in the same period came down marginally by 0.35%

title: Congress seeks high-level probe in alleged Ujjain land scam by Mohan Yadav family
content: Leader of Opposition in the Madhya Pradesh Legislative Assembly said that the land prices were increased in a “well planned manner” by bringing projects there, and land was purchased in those areas before announcing the projects.

title: Two arrested for defrauding software engineer over customs scam
content: Police seize ₹2 lakh in cash, three mobile phones, 57 bank passbooks, 35 ATM cards, and 49 cheque books from the accused, who are currently residing in Hyderabad

title: Belagavi will get two Ministerial berths, says Minister
content: Whether a third legislator will be made a Minister will be left to the Congress high command, according to Satish Jarkiholi

title: Sonowal launches five initiatives at VOC Port to advance green, digital and educational goals
content: Projects include PortGPT app, sustainability report, maritime logistics partnership and Kendriya Vidyalaya programme

title: Seethathodu, gateway to Gavi, unveils grand tourism plan
content: The KSEB, in association with the Seethathodu grama panchayat, is set to roll out an ambitious series of tourism diversification projects linked to the Gavi Tourism Circuit

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: CAG flags Kerala’s borrowing predicament
content: Of the total borrowings of ₹3.22 lakh crore in 2024-25, 86% (₹2.77 lakh crore) went towards repayment of earlier borrowings and ₹27,997.23 crore (9%) towards revenue expenditure. The net capital expenditure from the borrowings totalled ₹17,188.42 crore.

title: El Niño may trigger coral bleaching, hit fish catch: INCOIS
content: Special bulletin mentions the developing Pacific warming event may peak this winter, and sea surface temperatures in the Indian Ocean are likely to remain higher than normal until April-May 2027

title: Vijay government moves Supreme Court on Thirupparankundram row
content: The High Court’s Division Bench had earlier confirmed a previous Single Judge Bench order directing the Subramaniya Swamy Temple management to light the Karthigai Deepam at the ‘deepathoon’

title: Collector sets July 1 deadline to finish 2,103 Jaladhara works in Nandyal district
content: Of the total works sanctioned, 1,587 are completed and 516 are still pending; a Central Water Commission team will verify the structures built under NREGS

title: How safe is the knife that slices your bread? Hyderabad activist’s four-year battle leads to FSSAI order on rusted food cutting equipment
content: The advisory, issued by the FSSAI on June 15, directs Food Business Operators to use only food-grade, corrosion-resistant knives, blades and cutting equipment in food handling and processing operations

title: Bidadi agitation expands debate on development, food security and livelihoods
content: Farmer leader Badagalapura Nagendra calls this project a ‘Bengaluru-centric’ development, which has been consuming fertile agricultural land on the city’s outskirts

title: Hundreds of farmers join ‘Bairamangala Chalo’ rally against Bidadi Township Project
content: The rally marks the first large-scale farmers protest against the project, although local protests are ongoing for over 460 days.

title: Symposium explores humour’s role in challenging social and political realities
content: As part of the programme, a special session titled “Priyadarshan at 100” paid tribute to filmmaker Priyadarshan

title: Srisailam temple staff told to maintain cleanliness, hygiene at Kalyanakatta
content: Temple trust board chairman conducts surprise inspection of the tonsuring centre and emphasises the need for continuous sanitation measures at the facility

title: India needs to move from ease and cost to speed of doing business, CII president Mukundan says
content: In an interview to The Hindu, Mr. R. Mukundan pointed out several points of “friction” involving bureaucratic processes or logistics. He added that the private sector was indeed investing, contrary to allegations by government officials

title: 76% health insurance reimbursement claimants face immediate financial burden: report
content: Ability to manage costs only with liquid savings dropped from 32% to 24% in 2024-25 while financial stress faced by insurance holders at the time of raising claims has gone up across all tiers of cities between 2023 and 2025, says consumer insights report by online marketplace Policybazaar

title: Fisheries and Food Safety dept. in Kerala launch ‘Operation Sagar Rani’ to ensure quality of fish
content: Officials urge consumers to examine fish carefully before purchase, check its firmness and colour of eyes to detect deterioration; surprise checks being held at harbours, boat landing centres, and online stores

title: Pune trekker's death: Fiancée, her lover pushed him into gorge, say cops; both arrested
content: During interrogation, it emerged that since Agarwal was becoming an obstacle in Mr. Chaudhary’s relationship with Ms. Goyal, both of them plotted to eliminate her fiancé

title: KPHB Metro station gets police outpost amid rising complaints of solicitation, drug peddling and harassment
content: The outpost would provide immediate assistance to metro passengers facing harassment, stalking, disputes or other safety concerns

title: After Lucknow blaze, 14 coaching centres sealed in Jaipur; notices served to over 24
content: Teams of the municipal corporation and police carried out inspections across the city to verify fire safety arrangements, including the availability of firefighting equipment, emergency exits and mandatory fire no objection certificates (NOCs)

title: Fire accidents since Independence – A timeline
content: From The Hindu’s archives, a look at some of major fire accidents across the country since Independence

title: 15 die in Lucknow building fire, victims mostly students; three building owners arrested
content: Videos show youth falling from the building in Lucknow next to roadside railing; State govt forms SIT to probe incident; PM expresses grief, announces ₹2 lakh for kin of the deceased

title: Sharmila opposes TDP government’s “attempts to privatise” APSRTC
content: Backing the JAC stir, the Congress leader says e-buses should not go to private operators, and wants APSRTC to buy and own them

title: Why Kerala’s low-alcohol liquor policy has sparked a political storm | Explained
content: Kerala government’s decision to allow sale and production of low-alcohol beverages by fixing a sales tax for low-alcohol liquor under two different tax brackets has snowballed into a major controversy with allegations of corruption

title: Sudheeran writes to CM, asks him not to lower tax on low-spirit liquor
content: The former KPCC president also asks Satheesan to shelve the proposal to allow private investment in mineral mining

title: YSRCP has no moral right to preach political values: Tourism Minister
content: Durgesh says coalition government is handling Sai Krishna case transparently; accuses YSRCP of divisive caste politics and neglecting Kapu welfare during its tenure

title: Church bodies oppose move to reduce tax on low-alcohol beverages
content: State Committee of the Temperance Commission under the Kerala Catholic Bishops’ Council opposes the proposal and describes it as an ‘anti-Gen Z’ move

title: Kerala Assembly: LDF Opposition walks out over low-alcohol liquor tax row
content: LDF brought the issue to Assembly’s attention through Rule 50 notice. But Speaker refused to admit adjournment motion, ruling that the issue did not meet the level of urgency required to suspend House proceedings for detailed discussion

title: SIT grills suspended CI Nagaraju in Sai Krishna case
content: Investigation officers also question Task Force police of NTR Commissionerate, who reportedly picked up the victim from Markapuram

title: Kerala High Court upholds transfer of K.J. Reena from Director of Health Services post
content: The State contends that the government order transferring her was in accordance with norms, especially since the tenure of Dr. Reena’s deputation as the DHS had expired in February, 2026

title: Telegram access restored after High Court declines to stay blocking order
content: Internet providers and telecom operators started resuming access to the messaging app after a week-long block aimed at arresting fake NEET paper leak scams

title: Israel, US parting ways? Netanyahu says Iran war 'not over', seeks arms independence
content: Speaking during a meeting with reserve combat officers in Gush Etzion in the occupied West Bank, Netanyahu stressed the need for Israel to strengthen its domestic defence industry despite continued support from Washington. "I greatly appreciate the support we have received - and that I have secured over the years -- from our American friends. But today I say, we need our own independent weapons-production system. We must manufacture our own armaments."

title: Rohit Sharma conferred Padma Shri for outstanding contribution to Indian cricket
content: Cricket icon Rohit Sharma has been honored with the prestigious Padma Shri for his significant contributions to Indian cricket. President Droupadi Murmu presented the award, recognizing Sharma's stellar career as a batsman and captain. His leadership guided India to the 2024 T20 World Cup and subsequent Champions Trophy glory, solidifying his legacy as one of the nation's most accomplished cricketers.

title: 'Attempt to deflect': India snubs Khawaja Asif's 'will go to war over Indus' remark
content: MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal also spoke on the ongoing protest in the Pakistan Occupied Kashmir (PoK), saying the agitation was a  "direct consequence" of "systemic economic exploitation, denial of fundamental rights and administrative oppression in areas under its illegal and forcible occupation".​​ Earlier, Khawaja Asif had issued a warning to India, saying Islamabad could resort to military action if its water security comes under threat.

title: Oracle layoffs: Company confirms how many employees got 6AM email from leadership
content: Oracle has initiated significant layoffs globally, impacting thousands of employees across US, India, and other regions.  These workforce reductions, attributed to management changes, AI adoption, and strategic shifts, resulted in a 13% drop in its workforce.  The company incurred substantial restructuring costs, with severance packages reportedly less generous than industry peers.  This move comes amidst broader concerns about AI's impact on tech jobs.

title: Why the Middle East still beats all other options in India’s energy basket
content: India's energy sector is closely watching the Strait of Hormuz situation, highlighting a continued reliance on the Middle East for crucial LPG supplies. While alternative sources like the US were tapped during disruptions, they couldn't fully bridge the gap. Higher LNG prices also impacted demand, with a return to more favorable rates expected to boost consumption. This crisis underscores India's energy sourcing vulnerabilities.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
content: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

title: 'How can you hate Vaibhav Sooryavanshi?': Ashwin explodes at critics
content: Veteran spinner Ravichandran Ashwin has come to the defense of young cricketer Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, urging against excessive criticism following an on-field incident. Ashwin emphasized the need to differentiate between constructive feedback and personal attacks, highlighting that Sooryavanshi is still developing. He encouraged allowing the talented youngster to play freely, free from undue pressure, as he prepares for a potential India debut.

title: President Murmu confers Padma awards: Shibu Soren, Alka Yagnik, Mammootty among honourees
content: President Droupadi Murmu presented Padma Awards at Rashtrapati Bhavan, honouring 65 distinguished individuals. The ceremony recognized achievements across various fields, including arts, sports, and medicine. Notable recipients included actor Mammootty, singer Alka Yagnik, and cricketer Rohit Sharma. Dr. Padma Gurmet, awarded for his work in preserving Himalayan medical traditions, expressed immense pride for his community.

title: R Madhavan conferred with Padma Shri, bows down to PM Narendra Modi
content: President Murmu bestowed the remaining Padma Awards 2026, including Padma Bhushan for Mammootty and Alka Yagnik, at Rashtrapati Bhavan.  Prime Minister Modi and other dignitaries attended the ceremony where 65 honours were presented.  Notable recipients also included R Madhavan and Rohit Sharma, while veteran actor Satish Shah was honoured posthumously.  This follows an earlier ceremony where Dharmendra received a posthumous Padma Vibhushan.

title: After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM now replaces more than 1,000 workers with 50 robots
content: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.

title: Pune businessman’s son pushed into gorge: How fiancée tried to pass it off as 'accident'
content: A trek to Lohagad Fort, initially thought to be a tragic accident, has been revealed as a murder. Police arrested a woman and her alleged lover for allegedly pushing her fiancé into a deep gorge. The victim, Ketan Vishal Agrawal, was lured to the fort under the guise of birthday celebrations. Investigators uncovered a conspiracy driven by the woman's alleged unwillingness to marry and her relationship with another man.

title: Trump waives sanctions on Iran crude oil: What it means for India
content: As part of a broader package designed to provide economic relief to Iran, the US Treasury announced a sanctions waiver valid until August 21. The exemption allows Tehran to export oil and related products and receive payments for those sales.

title: Trouble mounts for Telegram: Govt report flags concern over child abuse, fraud
content: During the legal proceedings, Telegram faced accusations of failing to adequately curb misinformation spread through its channels, allegations the company has rejected. While the ban was lifted on Tuesday, users will remain unable to access the platform's message-editing feature until June 30. India is Telegram's largest market with more than 150 million users.

title: Tom Brady sends clear message after Gisele’s alleged Father’s Day snub
content: Tom Brady, the seven time Super Bowl champion, has had a pretty successful career but his past marriage with ex wife, Gisele Bundchen, has continued to dominate headlines even after years of their divorce. The Brazilian supermodel Gisele Bundchen recently made a controversial post for Father’s Day and it seems the NFL legend Tom Brady has made it clear what his current priorities are.

title: New Champions crowned, shocking betrayal headlines explosive WWE Raw Night
content: London's O2 Arena witnessed a dramatic WWE RAW go-home show for Night of Champions.  A beloved star turned heel, shocking fans, while Chad Gable faced his former stablemates.  Key bouts saw LA Knight triumph over Jimmy Uso amidst Bloodline interference, and The Street Profits capture the WWE World Tag Team Titles in a chaotic main event, culminating in a surprise championship coronation.

title: Who is Geno Smith’s alleged Ex? Inside the claims stirring NFL attention
content: The New York Jets’ star player, Geno Smith, was gearing up for the next season when he found himself in major legal trouble after an alleged ex girlfriend accused him of domestic violence and being neglectful of a child just a few hours ago. The controversial accusations have sparked shockwaves across the league and many are curious about Geno Smith’s alleged ex girlfriend.

title: Mammootty honoured with Padma Bhushan: President confers award, family joins ceremony
content: Veteran actor Mammootty was honored with the Padma Bhushan, India's third-highest civilian award, by President Droupadi Murmu. The prestigious ceremony, held at Rashtrapati Bhavan, saw his wife Sulfath and son Dulquer Salmaan by his side, celebrating his five decades of remarkable contributions to Indian cinema. This recognition underscores his enduring legacy and widespread impact across multiple film industries.

title: Syama Prasad at the centre of Bengal's new political story: BJP's cultural reset
content: The BJP is actively reshaping Bengal's historical narrative, emphasizing Syama Prasad Mookerjee's role in the state's Partition. By designating June 20 as West Bengal Day and celebrating Mookerjee's legacy, the party aims to correct perceived historical neglect. Critics argue this selective focus distorts a complex past, overlooking the broader political forces and human cost of Partition.

title: Not Kohli or Rohit: Jofra Archer picks this India star as the toughest batter
content: England pacer Jofra Archer surprisingly named KL Rahul as the most challenging batter he's faced in the IPL. Archer highlighted Rahul's prime form, stating the Indian batter's ability to score freely anywhere on the ground made him incredibly difficult to bowl to. This high praise from a premier fast bowler underscores Rahul's exceptional batting prowess during his peak years in the league.

title: Bengaluru techie, her boyfriend kill parents, sister after EMI notices reach home
content: A shocking crime unfolded in Bengaluru's Seegehalli, where a woman and her boyfriend allegedly murdered her parents and younger sister. The brutal act, believed to stem from a personal dispute, occurred late Monday night. Police are investigating the elder daughter, Shwetha, and her partner, Keneth, as prime suspects in the gruesome killings before they fled the residence.

title: Not just Japan: Rui Hachimura wants Asia's basketball dream to grow bigger
content: As teenagers from across Asia gathered inside Singapore's OCBC Arena for the NBA Rising Stars Invitational, Los Angeles Lakers forward Rui Hachimura made it clear that his vision extends beyond Japan. The first Japanese player drafted in the first round of the NBA Draft wants to inspire "the whole Asia" and create more pathways to the league, with Lauren Jackson and Hall of Famer Mitch Richmond echoing the importance of opportunities and grassroots development.

title: Vijay's mother recalls how he became 'quieter' after his sister's death
content: Vijay, the newly appointed Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu and a renowned actor, is often seen as the archetype of the protective brother on-screen. This character resonance stems from a profound personal loss—the death of his younger sister at just two years old. His mother shared that this tragedy shaped Vijay's personality, making him quieter and more introspective.

title: Jat Regiment troops participate in an exercise with soldiers from 18 countries in Mongolia
content: India's Jat Regiment is actively participating in Exercise Khaan Quest 2026 in Mongolia, a crucial multilateral drill focused on UN peacekeeping operations. This exercise, involving 18 nations, enhances interoperability and readiness for complex global missions. The participation aligns with India's strong strategic partnership with Mongolia and its significant contributions to international peace efforts, reinforcing its role as a responsible global player.

title: 'Bibi is hysterical about it': How US-Iran understanding is rattling Israel over Lebanon
content: Israel expresses deep concern over new US-Iran understandings potentially legitimizing Tehran's influence in Lebanon and restricting Israeli military actions against Hezbollah. Concerns arise from recent talks and a memorandum of understanding that Israeli officials believe may increase scrutiny on Israeli strikes and pressure for troop withdrawal, potentially weakening prior agreements.

title: FOMO about US stock market? How Indians can invest - routes, benefits, risks & tax implications
content: Apple, Microsoft, Nvidia and Amazon don’t trade on Indian exchanges, and for investors looking to diversify beyond the rupee and beyond Indian equities, the US market remains the deepest and most liquid in the world.

title: New World No. 1! Charani dethrones England's Smith to top Women's T20I bowling ranking
content: India's Sree Charani has become the No. 1 ranked bowler in Women's T20Is after an outstanding Women's T20 World Cup campaign. The 21-year-old tops the ICC rankings after taking 10 wickets in three matches, including match-winning spells against the Netherlands and South Africa. Charani displaced England's Linsey Smith, while Shafali Verma and Harmanpreet Kaur also climbed the batting rankings.

title: Why did stock market crash today? Sensex plunges 900 points - top reasons for fall
content: Around Rs 4.61 lakh crore in investor wealth was wiped as the combined market capitalisation of BSE-listed companies dropped to about Rs 475 lakh crore. BSE Sensex ended around 900 points down, dropped to levels just above 76,200. Nifty50 also dropped below 23,850.

title: 'Eetha' teaser: Shraddha Kapoor shines as pregnant Lavani icon Vithabai in biopic
content: Director Laxman Utekar's 'Eetha' stars Shraddha Kapoor as legendary Tamasha artiste Vithabai Narayangaonkar. The teaser, which premiered with 'Cocktail 2', shows Vithabai giving birth backstage mid-performance and returning to the stage — a true story of extraordinary courage. The film releases worldwide on August 28, 2026.

title: IPL trade: Pant returns to DC with Rs 12 crore cut, Kuldeep joins LSG
content: The IPL confirmed the move on Tuesday, with Pant agreeing to a salary of Rs 15 crore, Rs 12 crore less than the record Rs 27 crore deal he received when he joined Lucknow Super Giants ahead of the 2025 season.

title: From record highs to a sharp fall: South Korea’s market plunged 10%, here's why
content: South Korea's stock market experienced a dramatic plunge, with the KOSPI tumbling nearly 10% after hitting record highs. Investors rushed to sell chip stocks, particularly Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, amid concerns of excessive speculation and the impact of leveraged investment products. This sharp sell-off follows a strong year for the market, with attention now turning to US chipmaker Micron's earnings for sector direction.

title: Employee absent for 24 days won reinstatement from lower courts; SC restores dismissal
content: The Supreme Court has ruled that employees must update their permanent address with employers. An employee who failed to do so and claimed non-receipt of a disciplinary notice was denied reinstatement. The court emphasized that employers can only communicate using provided addresses and that employees bear the responsibility for informing changes.

title: Eyes in the sky: How low-cost aerostats can help plug India’s radar coverage gaps
content: India is enhancing its air defence with innovative solutions beyond traditional AWACS. Drones like the MQ-9B offer persistent surveillance, detecting low-flying threats. However, aerostat-based radar systems are emerging as a more cost-effective and dedicated option. These tethered balloons can provide continuous, wide-area coverage, complementing existing assets to create a robust, layered early warning network against modern aerial challenges.

title: Gavaskar pushes for rest window, says player rotation is 'cheapening the India cap'
content: His comments come after India completed a 3-0 ODI series sweep over Afghanistan, shortly after the conclusion of the Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026 season.

title: Chandigarh homes get costlier: How new collector rates affect property registration
content: As of April 1, homebuyers in Chandigarh will encounter elevated costs linked to new collector rates for properties. These adjustments, notably affecting prime real estate, result in increased stamp duty and registration fees. Even if the purchase price remains unchanged, buyers must anticipate higher registration costs stemming from the government's revised minimum property valuations.

title: US-Iran MoU outlines $300 billion Iran reconstruction fund — but who will pay the bill?
content: US secretary of state Marco Rubio is heading to the Gulf amid controversy over a proposed $300 billion reconstruction fund for Iran. While critics decry it as a US payout, the administration claims Gulf states and private investors would largely finance it. However, some Arab allies fear a wealthier Iran could boost its regional influence and military might, sparking unease despite suggestions of significant foreign interest in investing.

title: What is “quiet cracking”? Why more parents are struggling in silence
content: Parents are increasingly experiencing 'quiet cracking,' a silent burnout where they maintain outward functionality while feeling overwhelmed internally. This phenomenon stems from immense pressure from work, home, and emotional labor, exacerbated by societal expectations and a lack of support. The article highlights the need for reduced expectations, genuine connection, and practical backup to help parents cope and prevent this widespread exhaustion.

title: 'Engine of loot': Congress alleges MP CM Mohan Yadav involved in corruption, cites media report
content: "Mohan Yadav, has become the mastermind of this loot. There is also talk that the "farming of news" against the chief minister has been orchestrated by the agriculture minister who went from MP to the center! It seems like infighting over the chair and shares in the loot," Congress leader Jairam Ramesh said in a post on X.

title: CUET UG result 2026 declared, download score cards here
content: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has declared the CUET UG Result 2026, enabling candidates to download their score cards from the official website. Conducted for over 15.68 lakh registered candidates, the examination will determine undergraduate admissions for the 2026-27 academic session across participating universities. The NTA has also released key examination statistics, performance highlights and topper details. Candidates should now download their score cards and complete university-specific admission and counselling formalities.

title: T.N. CM Vijay cites 2021 Assembly centenary event to counter Opposition over National Anthem row
content: Mr. Vijay said that during the centenary celebrations of the Legislative Assembly, an event presided over by the then President of India, it was announced the programme would begin with the “National Anthem followed by the Tamil Thaai Vazhthu”

title: "B Team Of Disruptive Elements": Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan To NDTV On CJP
content: Those who were rejected in the democracy have come in disguise and are now after the system, said Dharmendra Pradhan, slamming the CJP.

title: Hormuz Ship Traffic Highest Since Start Of Middle East War: Report
content: At least 37 commodity carriers transited the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, a record level since the start of the Middle East war in late February, according to data from the maritime tracking firm Kpler.

title: Fiancee's Failed Attempt 4 Days Before Murder, A Snake Excuse, And A Hug
content: Vishal Agarwal, father of the 26-year-old real estate director, has described how Siya pushed Ketan from the fort on June 14. When Ketan survived by grabbing a bush, Siya shouted that a snake was approaching and then hugged him.

title: "Left Me On My Birthday": Woman's Instagram Story After Killing Fiance
content: Siya shared an emotional Instagram story mourning his death. In the post, she expressed deep grief and posed questions about his sudden departure.

title: Iran, Oman Plan Talks on Hormuz Control, Service Charges
content: Oman and Iran will study the future administration of the Strait of Hormuz and the costs to be charged for services provided, they said in a joint statement Tuesday, insisting on sovereignty over the vital waterway.

title: Adani Mundra Airport Begins Commercial Ops, Inaugural Flight Lands From Goa
content: Earlier in the day, regional carrier Star Air became the first airline to start scheduled commercial flights at the airport, with the inaugural flight landing from Goa.

title: How 'Witches' Of Ukraine Are Haunting Russians, One Drone Strike At A Time
content: Over four years, the war in Ukraine has changed, and with it, its people and tactics. It is now fought in the steady accumulation of small pieces of information that eventually become a set of coordinates and, shortly afterwards, a drone strike.

title: Eiffel Tower To Close Early Due To Severe Heatwave
content: The Eiffel Tower, one of the most visited tourist attractions in the world, was on Tuesday set to close early because of a severe heatwave, its operator said.

title: Video: Truck Falls In River After Bridge Collapses In Himachal, Driver Safe
content: The incident occurred between Reckong and Tapri on National Highway 5 (Old Hindustan-Tibet Road) near Urni Dhank when the truck was passing the bridge.

title: Birthday Bash On Assam Bridge Lands 14 In Legal Trouble, Luxury Cars Seized
content: A late-night birthday celebration held on the Kumar Bhaskar Varma Bridge has sparked legal action after videos of the event circulated widely on social media, drawing criticism over public safety and misuse of a vital transport corridor.

title: Delhi Colony Keeps Trash Out Of Landfills, Shows Way With 'Zero-Waste' Model
content: The model focuses on managing waste within the community instead of sending it directly to landfill sites.

title: "I Felt Jumping Was Only Option": Survivor Recounts Lucknow Fire Horror
content: By the time the door finally opened, thick smoke had begun filling the floor. The staircase -- their only escape route -- had already become a tunnel of smoke and flames.

title: Forgotten Warnings, Delayed Reforms: Why Coaching Hubs Are Vulnerable To Fire
content: An NDTV reality check in South Delhi's Kalu Sarai and Neb Sarai - two of the capital's largest coaching centre clusters - suggests that many of the same safety concerns continue to exist despite repeated warnings, inquiries and promises of reform.

title: Tarique Rahman Begins China Visit, Teesta Project Talks On The Agenda
content: On 24 June, Tarique Rahman will hold a meeting with the prime minister of Kazakhstan and later depart Dalian for Beijing by high-speed train later in the day. Rahman will also meet leaders of other countries on the trip.

title: 'Wife Looking For Husband': Udhayanidhi Stalin's Attack After Vijay's 'Story'
content: U Stalin, who is the opposition leader in the Tamil Nadu Assembly, mentioned that the state knows the "story of the wife looking for a husband in the Chengalpattu court", a reference to Vijay's wife Sangkeetha Sornalingam filing the divorce petition in the Chengalpattu court.

title: Trump Says Unfrozen Iran Funds To Go Into Escrow Account Controlled By US
content: US President Donald Trump said Tuesday that any Iranian funds unfrozen through sanctions relief or released by the US Treasury would go into a Washington-controlled escrow account, which Tehran could tap to purchase US food and medical supplies.

title: Every AI Prompt Costs Water. Nvidia's 'Liquid Coolant' Tech Could Change That
content: As AI systems become more powerful, the data centres that run them require enormous amounts of energy and cooling. The United Nations has warned that by the end of this decade, AI data centres could consume as much water as 1.3 billion people.

title: Opinion: The AI Overhaul: What India Can Learn From China's Massive University Shakeup
content: China has carried out the most sweeping university overhaul in the past five years. It has eliminated 12,200 undergraduate programmes and introduced 10,200 new ones. We in India need to sit with this news for a moment.

title: "Very Unfortunate": India Condoles Death Of 12 Indians In Qatar Gas Plant Explosion
content: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) said that authorities are working closely to ensure the identification and are in talks with local authorities for the transportation of their mortal remains.

title: Tamil Nadu Approaches Supreme Court Against High Court Order On Deepam Row
content: The C Joseph Vijay-led state government had moved the apex court on June 11 and filed the petition through the Tamil Nadu government counsel B Karunakaran.

title: Why AI sounds certain even when it’s wrong and why we believe it
content: Ananya Podder, a news intern at the Times of India, is a political science major who completed a PG Diploma from the Indian Institute of Mass Communication. Currently working across diverse beats. Read More

title: Mamata hits back at rebels: TMC expels Firhad Hakim, 7 others over 'anti-party activities'
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics.
